First, that they must put other people's needs ahead of their own; second, that they must give in order to get; and third, that they must earn a place in the affections of others because love will not simply be given to them.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: This orientation creates a major problem for Twos, however. To fully identify with the role of nurturer and to maintain the positive feelings that this role creates for them, Twos must deeply repress their own needs, hurts, and self-doubt.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: In the average range, Self-Preservation Twos repress their own Self-Preservation instincts while focusing on taking care of the needs of others. They are the Instinctual Variant most likely to wear themselves out for people while ignoring their own needs, often failing to get adequate rest or time for themselves.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: In the average range, the Social instinct expresses itself in Twos as a powerful desire to be liked and approved by everyone in their social sphere. They (like Sevens) usually maintain a busy social calendar and enjoy introducing people, networking, and hosting get-togethers.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: In the average range, Sexual Twos are the true intimacy junkies of the Enneagram. They are driven to get closer to others, both emotionally and physically. Sexual Twos like to win over people who are attractive to them, especially if these people present a challenge or seem initially uninterested.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Twos let go of the belief that they are not allowed to care for themselves. Self-Nurturing Thus they can own their feelings and needs and are free to love others Unconditionally without expectations.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Twos tend to be very generous, but they also tend to fall prey to insecurities about others' affections for them. If they begin to fear that whatever good they have been doing for others is not enough, they can begin to get caught up in 'people-pleasing'—looking for things to say and do that will make people like them.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: everything in me really wantedtogive ittohersothatshe would like me and be myfriend.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: AverageTwosbegintodefinethemselves astheSpecialFriendor theConfidante. Theywantotherstoregardthemastheirbestfriend and toseekthemoutforadviceand tosharespecialsecretsandinti-

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Pridebetraysitselfin the defensiveness thatariseswhensomeonehas theaudacitytosuggestthatTwosdoindeedhaveneedsandhurts.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Twosunconsciously judgethe responses ofothers,and only afewselectactionsget through their superego filter.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Bestowing approval, compliments, applause, andflatterycanbese ductivetoothers,andaverageTwosknowit.They knowthe powerof positiveattention and howstarvedfor it most peopleare.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: TheSocialand SexualInstinctual Variants canmaketheiremotional andsexualneeds known rather clearlyandforcefully, whether or not the other person wantsthat kindofinteraction.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Twoshave learned that they cannot expresstheir needs and de mandsdirectly—they must do so indirectly, hoping that others will pick up the hints and repay them in various ways.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: On thepositiveside,Twos'emotional andempathetic connections withothersmakethemgenuinely wanttodowhatevertheycantohelp someoneindistress,whiletheirgenerosity andenergyenablethemto followthroughintangibleways.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: If Twos continue to overextend themselves for others, they wear themselves out physically as well as emotionally and financially. Their health inevitably begins to suffer because they are also "stuffing their feelings" (somatizing), producing eating disorders, weight gain, psychosomatic illnesses, and/or substance abuses.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: When their anxieties and stress exceed their coping abilities, Twos go to Eight, becoming more blunt and forceful. Twos normally present an image of selfless kindness, but the move to Eight reveals that they are remarkably tough underneath—others discover that beneath the velvet glove is an iron fist.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Your pride is a compensation for something else: an underlying fear of worthlessness, that nobody wants you. Work on your pride by first seeing the many ways in which it subtly manifests itself.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Healthy Twos are able to distinguish their own needs from the needs of others and to maintain a healthy balance between the two. Louise comments: I am at my best when I am at peace with myself. I can feel what I need and say it directly.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: It is a major healing for Twos to realize that they are not going to heal their hearts this way—no matter how many self-sacrifices they make. They can, however, turn to the one source that can fulfill them—their own Essential nature.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: On a very deep level, Twos remember the Essential quality of unconditional love and the omnipresence of love. When they remember their Essential nature and the Divine state that it mirrors, healthy Twos are aware of the presence of love all around them.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: "You are good or okay if you are loved by others and close to them." "Why does your value depend on someone loving you, and how do you know if they do? Even if they don't, what has that got to do with you? Twos struggle to get closer to others but still feel unloved."

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Maybe I could let someone else do this. Maybe this person is actually already showing me love in their own way. Maybe I could do something good for myself, too.
